claim: The framework body (the Go `internal/` package tree) shall be owned by no single agent -- it is shared infrastructure any agent's code may import.
why: Second half of the split R-agent-imports-framework (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). This is a non-code ownership/governance stance about the framework body -- distinct from the mechanically-checkable import-direction fact already split out as R-agent-code-imports-framework. No check_* can verify 'owned by no single agent' -- ownership is a governance convention (no per-agent CODEOWNERS-style claim exists anywhere in the repo, and none should), so this stays honestly STRUCTURAL/INHERENTLY_PROSE, carried by the fact that the Go framework package (internal/) has exactly one owner field (framework-author, the framework's own resolver) and no agent directory declares or claims exclusive ownership of any internal/ module. In the Go port the framework body is the `internal/` package tree (module github.com/PHPCraftdream/HotamSpecGo), replacing the Python-era hotam_spec.* package. Landed as the missing REPLACES target flagged in the Wave 2 review. REPOINTED 2026-07-02 (Wave 7 move 4): assumptions moved from A-content-free-honest to A-framework-shared-infra-no-owner, which names this requirement's actual premise.
